title: Fix admin panel blog post display and editing

requirements.text: Fix the admin panel (AdminPostsPage) so that all blog posts are fetched and displayed correctly in the posts list table, including posts created via the Create Blog Post form.

quotes: how to edit this as it nit showing in asdmin page
Fix the admin panel so posts display and can be edited properly

acceptanceCriteria: All blog posts (published and draft) appear in the admin panel list when navigating to the admin page
Each post row shows the post title, category, publish status, and action buttons (edit, delete, toggle publish)
The edit button navigates to the EditBlogPostPage for the selected post
The delete button triggers a confirmation dialog and removes the post on confirmation
The toggle publish button correctly switches a post between published and draft states
Loading and empty states are handled gracefully with appropriate messages

requirements.text: Ensure the admin panel uses the correct React Query hook that fetches ALL blog posts (including unpublished drafts), not just published posts, so admins can manage all content.

quotes: how to edit this as it nit showing in asdmin page

acceptanceCriteria: AdminPostsPage calls the hook that retrieves all visible posts (useGetAllPosts or equivalent), not the published-only hook
Draft posts are visible in the admin list and are visually distinguished from published posts (e.g., a 'Draft' badge)

requirements.text: Verify and fix the backend getAllPosts (or equivalent) method to correctly return all blog posts to admin callers, ensuring the Motoko actor's access control does not accidentally filter out posts for anonymous or non-admin callers when accessed from the admin panel.

quotes: how to edit this as it nit showing in asdmin page

acceptanceCriteria: The backend exposes a method that returns all blog posts regardless of publish status
The method can be called from the frontend admin panel without requiring Internet Identity authentication to simply list posts
CRUD operations (update, delete, toggle publish) remain protected and require admin privileges

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Keep all Motoko backend logic in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Adding new blog post fields or changing the data model
Redesigning the admin panel UI beyond fixing the display issue
Adding new admin features beyond post CRUD
